what_it_does: Proposes a new face dna/budgets.json that maps 12 empirical canonical content slots (plus 2 inferred decision-surface slots) to per-slot character/word/line budgets and recommended caps, with formulas derived from assumed canvas, type sizes, and per-weight char-advance values.
observations: Normalizes 293 raw content samples / 261 distinct authored slot names across 8 source collections into 12 canonical slots.
Provides min/median/max chars, words, and observed line counts per canonical slot, attributed to source collections.
Distinguishes one-line slots (slide-title, section-head, stat-figure, button/cta, person-name, node-label) from multi-line slots (subtitle, body, stat-label, bullet, caption, person-credential).
Gives each recommended cap as both a resolved integer and its derivation recipe so it can re-derive if canvas/type assumptions change.
Two slots (option-label, option-implication) have zero source samples and are explicitly flagged as inferred nearest-analog budgets.
References assumed canvas values: frame 1280-1600, content width 1424, char advance per weight, frame proportion title-main-footer.
Includes a proposed JSON shape for dna/budgets.json, computed rules, open questions for Tim, and a provenance table.
Notes reconciliation with type-lane anchors in grammar.json > scales.type.budget rather than contradicting them.
standouts: Every cap is presented as a formula plus a resolved integer, making the budget a function of canvas/type rather than a magic number.
Decision-surface slots are marked 'NO SOURCE SAMPLE' and raised as open questions, refusing to fabricate empirical data.
Body, subtitle, and bullet caps are tier-sensitive (compressed/standard/expanded) keyed to line-count rather than a single value.
Person-role is split into role-title (one-line) and credential/qualification (two-line) to account for a bimodal distribution.
The 'no-2-line-wrap law' is applied strictly to headings/labels/figures/buttons, with explicit carve-outs for intentional wrapping in caption/subtitle/body/bullet.
conventions: Collapses many raw slot names into canonical slot names with provenance lists.
Labels each slot's evidence as empirical or inferred.
Includes conflict/reconciliation notes that align caps with existing type-lane anchors.
Uses a JSON structure with augmentations, new_sections, computed_rules, open_questions_for_tim, and _provenance_table.
Cites script-computed, reproducible statistics and flags long-tail outliers routed to other roles.
concerns: Caps depend on assumed canvas values (1424px content width, char-advance midpoints) that are not themselves authoritative within this file.
The two inferred slots have no empirical data; their budgets are analog-based and provisional.
Several observed maxima are reclassified as long-tail outliers belonging to other roles, but the file does not prove those reclassifications.
The canonical frame assumption may not hold for web-scroll product canvases noted as scale evidence rather than fixed frame.
notes: A data-driven proposal to turn Tim's 'text has intentional length' law into generator-enforceable, re-derivable per-slot content budgets.
questions: Which canonical authoring frame should lock the resolved integer caps: 1280, 1600, or 1920?
Should stat-label and free caption remain split, or merge to match the type lane's single 'caption' bucket?
How should tier-sensitive caps for body/subtitle/bullet be keyed in budgets.json?
Are option-label and option-implication intended as one-line chips or as short card titles that may run two lines?
Is the credential sub-role allowed two lines on a team card, or should it truncate to one line?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to create a single home for per-slot content-budget numbers so the computed rule and generator do not diverge; the file does exactly that, while also supplying formulas, assumptions, open questions, and a schema shape.
apparent_themes: Empirical grounding of design law in machine-readable budgets.
Re-derivable caps over hardcoded magic numbers.
Separation of empirical evidence from inferred placeholders.
intention_signals: Trying to bridge design intent and generative code by giving each canonical text role a measurable, enforceable length budget that can be recomputed from canvas and type parameters.
novelty: Treats content length as a computed design substrate with per-weight char advance, zone-based cap recipes, tier-sensitive line budgets, and explicit inferred-vs-empirical provenance.
contribution: A structured, reproducible method for deriving and recording per-slot content budgets, including the one-line vs multi-line distinction, tier-sensitive caps, equal-height sibling constraints, and a template for budgets.json that separates empirical numbers from inferred placeholders.
summary_for_embedding: Proposed dna/budgets.json face that maps 12 empirical and 2 inferred canonical text slots to character/word/line budgets and re-derivable caps based on assumed canvas and type parameters.
intention_for_embedding: To give Tim's content-budget law a single machine-readable home so generators and checkers can enforce intentional text length per canonical slot.
